title: Odds Form: Log-Odds Add Linearly

runtime.expected_outcome: Consecutive independent observations shift log-odds by an additive amount equal to the log-likelihood ratio; graph reads as a sum of independent contributions.

math.latex:
\log  rac{P(s \mid o_{1:n})}{P(
eg s \mid o_{1:n})} = \log  rac{P(s)}{P(
eg s)} + \sum_{i=1}^{n} \log  rac{P(o_i \mid s)}{P(o_i \mid 
eg s)}

symbols.log-odds: signed log of the posterior ratio
symbols.prior odds: log ratio of P(s) to P(-s)

audiences.kid: Every clue adds or subtracts the same amount from a running score. If the score goes up, it means the clue pointed to yes; down means no.
audiences.real: In log-odds space, Bayes updates are addition: each independent piece of evidence contributes a signed log-likelihood ratio. Makes batched updates trivial.
audiences.equation: Define l_t = log P(s|o_{1:t}) / P(-s|o_{1:t}). Then l_t = l_0 + sum of log P(o_i|s) / P(o_i|-s). Linear in independent evidence.
audiences.derivation: Take the log of the Bayes update for both s and -s; subtract. Conditional independence of observations given the state makes the log-likelihood ratios additive (Jaynes 2003, Ch 4).

orchestrate.objective: Show that log-odds form turns Bayes into addition over independent evidence streams.
orchestrate.role: Numerical-literacy tutor connecting probability to information-theoretic intuitions.
orchestrate.context: A 3x3 maze where the agent accumulates several wall-signature observations without moving, so the log-odds graph stays interpretable.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 2.
Jaynes (2003), Probability Theory: The Logic of Science, Cambridge, Ch 4.
